title: lycianthes laevis subsp. kaitisis
intro: Lycianthes laevis subsp. kaitisis, also known as the New Zealand or Chinese potato bush, is a plant species belonging to the Solanaceae family, native to New Zealand and other parts of the Pacific Islands. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Lycianthes laevis subsp. kaitisis include reducing inflammation, fighting against infections, and providing relief from pain and fever. The plant is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction for its herbal remedies, and can also be used as a food ingredient due to its edible tubers.
